title: Greetings in English
description: Magic greetings that change your day!

scenes.speech: Greetings are the first step in English! Give people a smile! 👋
scenes.title: Start with a Greeting
scenes.content: This lesson is about English greetings. A short word shrinks the distance between you and someone you've just met.

scenes.speech: Hello works any time of day!
scenes.title: Hello — All-Purpose
scenes.content: Hello is the base greeting that fits any time. Try 'Hi there' when you want it slightly more friendly.

scenes.title: Morning, Noon, Night
scenes.content: Good morning, Good afternoon, Good evening. Matching the time of day makes a great impression.
scenes.highlight:
🌅 Good morning
☀️ Good afternoon
🌙 Good evening

scenes.speech: 'How are you?' is the friendship password!
scenes.title: How are you?
scenes.content: When asked, reply with 'I'm good' or 'Not bad'. Send it back with 'And you?' to keep the conversation alive.
scenes.highlight:
Q: How are you?
A: I'm good, and you?

scenes.speech: Learn farewells too — they're a pair!
scenes.title: Saying Goodbye
scenes.content: Goodbye is a bit formal. With friends, 'See you' or 'Bye' works fine. 'See you tomorrow' is handy when you'll meet again.
scenes.highlight: Bye / See you / See you tomorrow

scenes.title: Power of Thank You
scenes.content: You can say Thank you many times a day. Thanks is the casual version. For deeper gratitude, Thank you so much.
scenes.highlight: Thanks / Thank you / Thank you so much

scenes.title: Apologies
scenes.content: I'm sorry is the basic apology. After bumping into someone, just 'Sorry!' is enough. Add 'really' when you mean it deeper.
scenes.highlight: Sorry / I'm sorry / I'm really sorry

scenes.title: Smile and Eye Contact
scenes.content: English greetings carry as much in the face and eyes as in the mouth. With a smile and eye contact, the same Hello changes everything.
scenes.highlight: 🎯 Word + Smile + Eyes = Greeting that lands

scenes.title: Build a Greeting Habit
scenes.content: Greet your family or friends in English three times a day. The more reps, the more natural it gets.
scenes.highlight:
Morning: Good morning
Noon: How are you?
Night: Good night

scenes.speech: Quiz time! How do you reply to 'How are you?'?
scenes.quizQuestion: Which is the most natural reply to 'How are you?'
scenes.quizExplanation: Reply 'I'm good' (or similar), then bounce it back with 'And you?'.

scenes.speech: You're a greeting master from today! 🌟
scenes.title: Lesson Complete!
